issues.title: A brief review on draft-ietf-satp-usecases
issues.author: anawhj
issues.body:
Hello.

> (1.a)  Has the Document Shepherd personally reviewed this version of the document and, in particular, does he or she believe this version is ready for forwarding to the IESG for publication? (Ref. https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc4858.html#section-3.1)

According to Chair's call and confirmation at IETF 122 meeting, I'm supposed to be in charge of the document shepherd's role for draft-ietf-satp-usecases.
For handling (1.a) question above, I provide some minor comments for better degree of completion for the usecases draft.
Please also handle the previous comments shared via SATP mailing list before. (e.g. Does not have a table of contents in draft-ietf-satp-usecases, https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/sat/gtbhvvdhx6n08vUFqT713TuAC1c/)

**Minor comments (They can be applied to the draft selectively by authors)**
* “Internet Engineering Task Force” is specified at the first line of the draft, but  “Secure Asset Transfer Protocol” would be better in terms of the consistency with the satp-core draft and other WGs’ I-Ds.
* The draft includes seven figures that could be specified with a short caption right to “Figure” keyword.
* In Figure 7, dot is used for describing the meaning of a direction indicator, but the other figures have no dot on the same case. In other words, “1. Exercise” can be modified as “1 Exercise” in Figure 7.
* “consortiums and identified as challenges” → “consortiums, and identified as challenges” (In “1. Introduction“ section. a comma can be added for better sentence.)
* There following are → The following are (2. Terminology section. The current one would be no problem, but the proposed change would be better I think.)
* Asset Transfer Protocol → Secure Asset Transfer Protocol (2. Terminology section. There’s no case using the “Asset Transfer Protocol in the document, but “Secure Asset Transfer Protocol” is used.)
* Further terminology definitions can be found in [NIST] and [ISO]. (2. Terminology section. I’m not sure the statement is really needed in the document. I tried to find some terminology in the reference links, but it would be difficult to find something meaningful.)
* “E.g.,”, “e.g.” and “e.g.” are used in the document as mixed, so it can be unified as a single one.
* “payment network”, “payments network”, “payment networks”, “Payment Networks” are used in the document as mixed, so parts of them could be integrated as a single one.

**Trivial error (Please check them using Ctrl + F)**
* DeFi user market → DeFi user markets
* arbitrarily → arbitrary
* audio playback app. → audio playback application.
* user → users (for consistency)
* <[https://ethereum.org/”](https://ethereum.org/%E2%80%9D) → <[https://ethereum.org/”](https://ethereum.org/%E2%80%9D)> Let’s add “>”.

**Weird links to be updated**
* [https://www.hyperledger.org/use/besu](https://www.hyperledger.org/use/besu%3E) → https://www.lfdecentralizedtrust.org/projects/besu
* [https://www.hyperledger.org/blog/2022/11/07/introducing- hyperledger-cacti-a-multi-faceted-pluggable-interoperability- framework](https://https//www.hyperledger.org/blog/2022/11/07/introducing-%20hyperledger-cacti-a-multi-faceted-pluggable-interoperability-%20framework) → https://www.lfdecentralizedtrust.org/blog/2022/11/07/introducing-hyperledger-cacti-a-multi-faceted-pluggable-interoperability-framework
* https://www.iso.org/ → https://www.iso.org/standard/82208.html
* https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5730.html → https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5730 (For consistency)
* https://www.ibm.com/case-studies/we-trade-blockchain → https://www.ibm.com/case-studies/wetrade-blockchain-fintech-trade-finance
* https://www.ibm.com/blockchain/solutions/food-trust isn’t available now.
* https://marcopolonetwork.com/ isn’t available now.
* https://www.r3.com/reports/corda-technical-whitepaper isn’t available now.
* https://www.ibm.com/blogs/blockchain/2018/09/blockchain-for-kyc-game-changing-regtech-innovation isn’t available now.

comments.author: VRamakrishna
comments.body: @anawhj Thanks for the comments. We'll go through them in detail and apply the necessary changes. On the links containing `hyperledger`: the organization previously called Hyperledger was moved and renamed to Linux Foundation Decentralized Trust at the end of 2024, hence the links (originally added more then 2 years ago) are out-of-date. Thanks especially for pointing them out.

comments.author: VRamakrishna
comments.body:
@anawhj I've incorporated your feedback into the document and acknowledged you in the "Contributors" section at the end (please check if your affiliation is stated correctly): https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-satp-usecases/.

I still haven't figured out the table of contents, but I'll do that as soon as I can using the other SATP drafts' GitHub repos as examples, and using the XML conversion too.

comments.author: anawhj
comments.body: @VRamakrishna Great! The affiliation is correct for me. Thanks for mentioning me to the section. :)

comments.author: anawhj
comments.body: @VRamakrishna Could you briefly share the plan of the final update incl. the table of contents? If needed, I would be able to help the problem of the table of contents together.

comments.author: VRamakrishna
comments.body: @anawhj I'm working on it as we speak. Sorry for the procrastination. I'll make some commits this week (and seek help from you as needed). Thanks.

comments.author: anawhj
comments.body:
Great! Thanks for the info.
I will provide a shepherding document via the STAP mailing list once you confirm the final version of the draft.

comments.author: VRamakrishna
comments.body:
@anawhj I've checked in the draft and added a branch protection rule for PRs to `main`. (I uploaded all previous drafts, too, and tagged those commits by version number.)

You can now submit corrections and suggestions directly against the draft. A table of contents is also generated now. I have some newer changes pending, and whenever I upload a new draft to datatracker, I'll ensure it has the ToC by building it from this repo.

comments.author: anawhj
comments.body: @VRamakrishna Thanks for the update. Please submit it to the datatracker incl. IANA section shared from Claire via SATP mailing list.

comments.author: hardaker
comments.body: Thank you very much for tracking all of these issues and ensuring they get done!  Very much appreciated.

comments.author: anawhj
comments.body:
@VRamakrishna As last questions for describing the shepherd note, could you please check the following two items?

* There seems two errors on the validation result of the link below. Do we need to fix or just skip them? Please provide your opinion. (I'll add your opinion to the shepherd note.)
  * https://author-tools.ietf.org/api/idnits?url=https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-satp-usecases-06.txt

> 8. Describe reviews and automated checks performed to validate sections of the final version of the document written in a formal language, such as XML code, BNF rules, MIB definitions, CBOR's CDDL, etc.

* Regarding the shepherding checklist item 8 above, please share your document type (e.g. XML, md) and automated validation method (e.g. what types of tools)

Thanks again for your prompt response, indeed.
